text_clean: Thermals - A Hot Mess I ended up going with the M15 R4 RTX 3070. On paper the specs are very impressive, but at the end of the day, it only matters if the equipment is capable of utilizing these specs. If you're going to run AAA title games, your laptop is going to overheat and shut down unless you know how to undervolt it. It's pretty pitiful that you have handicap your laptop to run it, especially when factoring the price. It's absolutely lazy engineering by Advance. Even the most modest games will push the thermals north of 80 Celsius. Games like Back 4 Blood and Cyperpunk register consistently at 100 Celsius (which is the maximum temperature the laptop can safely run without damaging the hardware) until the laptop has a heat stroke and then dies. I bought a cooling pad just so that I can rest the laptop on my lap without singeing my poor pubes. There are videos online that'll teach you how to undervolt the laptop. I personally prefer ThrottleStop, but it's pathetic that I have to utilize it just to play the games that this laptop was supposedly built to play. Please read up on this issue on Reddit before you commit. This is a known issue with this laptop. I wish I had done some more research before purchasing this furnace in laptop's clothing.

title_x: Terrible trackpad, battery life, and temperature control
text_clean: Terrible trackpad, battery life, and temperature control As others have mentioned, the trackpad is atrocious and this computer has some serious temperature control issues. I actually bought this computer specifically for its large trackpad. Having used various MacBooks as my personal computer for the last decade plus, I’ve gotten very used to never carrying around a mouse and keyboard with me, due to the MacBook’s fantastic trackpad. So, when I was told I’d need a Windows laptop for work, I chose this one specifically for its large trackpad. Unfortunately, the trackpad is completely unusable. Unlike others, mine actually has no wobble to it, but it is incredibly erratic and inconsistent in response. The mouse hiccups and stutters all over the place when using the trackpad, whereas it’s super smooth when using a mouse. Luckily, I use a dock with mouse and keyboard most of the time when I’m in my office at work.  Hilariously, the FusionTech dock I purchased with it doesn’t actually work correctly, as I get a warning every single time the computer boots up telling me the dock it’s plugged into is not to correct spec to provide full power and functionality, despite it being the dock FusionTech sells for this very computer (the highest watt one). It works fine, honestly, but I get hit with the error constantly because this computer is ALWAYS shutting down due to low battery power overnight, despite putting it into hibernate (not sleep) mode every night before I put it in my bag. The battery life in general is not great, but the fact that neither sleep nor hibernate mode will prevent it from dying overnight is unacceptable.  Perhaps worst of all, for most people anyway, this thing has really serious temperature control problems. It frequently sounds like a jet engine (PS4 Pro owners will know the sound well) while performing pretty simple tasks (like using Microsoft Office apps or browsing the web) and more intensive tasks require messing around with the power and fan settings to avoid the computer suddenly going black from overheating, something I experienced many times when I tried to play Civ 6 on it.  I truly cannot believe that this laptop costs what it does and has the reviews it does, because it is borderline unusable for many applications. Unfortunately, I cannot afford to be without a work computer for long enough to have it looked at, so I’m stuck with this thing until it drives me crazy enough to buy something else.  Two stars (instead of just one) simply because it does have a really nice screen.

text_clean: Laptop has serious flaws. On paper, it looks like a great laptop, and games do run as you would expect them to. But there are numerous issues that ruin the experience. First, and foremost, there is sleep. FusionTech has completely disabled it in favor of connected standby. So unless you shut your computer down, you are likely to pull the laptop out of your bag with a dead battery and the entire thing hot to the touch. This is unacceptable for a portable device. Additionally, battery life is mediocre, even for a gaming laptop, and the thermals are poorly designed. Even just browsing the web will leave this laptop too hot to sit in your laptop. And during gaming sessions, the fans will blow hot air right over your hands making the experience uncomfortable. Frankly there are are too many better options at this price point. I would suggest looking at those.

text_clean: Decent computer with some issues I like it overall. However, there are some things that are REALLy f-ing annoying. This is long but hopefully informative.  1. The touch pad/track pad is crap. The sensitivity is horrible. So, bad and frustrating when you are trying to do a screen grab and hold the click while expanding the selection. It just decides your done for no reason and you end up wasting 10 minutes dicking around to get your screen grab. "Who uses a trackpad anyway?" you might ask. Valid question. On to my next point.  2. I use an external monitor for my work. It is a major productivity booster for me as a software engineer. This computer, as many these days, comes with a USB-C adapter to connect an HDMI monitor. Great so far. This adapter comes ready to accept regular USB connections as well. Perfect for connecting a wireless USB mouse. Except the mouse signal conflicts with the stupid monitor signal and introduces intermittent delay and unresponsiveness (I nearly threw it across the room). To fix this issue you must use two adapters, one for the monitor and one for the mouse. Not the best but at least it works.  3. I spent a fair amount of time traveling with my wife in our RV. Quarters are tight and when I want to have a meeting I need to be able to plug in a headset so I don't disturb her while she is working. I use a headset that has worked well with other computers and still works well on my phone. However, when I use it on this computer, people can barely hear me. No amount of fiddling with settings makes any difference. Fine, I'll use my phone and just listen to YouTube on this thing. Well, after you plug in the head set and remove it a couple times, it stops recognizing it all together. Solution: a F&**$&ng reboot.  Dear FusionTech, this is the most frustrating POS I have ever owned. I gave it 2 stars instead of 1 because I am sure not everyone uses machines in the same way I do. Trying to be fair. But F*$&^k me, this is true frustration.

title_x: A waste of specs and money
text_clean: A waste of specs and money When I first read the specs for the 5310 I was really intrigued. It sounded like a whole lot of machine in a very small package. But it doesn't take long to realize that the 5310 crams in a lot of unnecessary hardware and cost for virtually no improved performance.  CPU  The Intel Core i7-11370H. The idea behind this chip was to compete with Ryzen in the laptop/ultra portable gaming sector of the market. But after their previous 6 and 8 core 10 generation series the 11370H is a step back for Intel having only 4 cores, which for 2021 just isn't enough anymore.  The 11370H might shine in single threaded productivity tasks but that's where the pros of the chip end. Paired with a high end GPU (like in the Asus TUF Dash F15 which sports a 3070) the 11370H is passable in modern titles that aren't CPU bound, thought the performance is well behinds competitors. Unfortunately the Worktop 5310's GPU is far from high end.  GPU  NVIDIA claims the GeForce MX450 slots somewhere in between a 1050 and a 1650. Frankly I don't know enough to be able to compare the three GPUs but my experience with the MX450 is I need to know to come to a conclusion here. For the most part, modern games are unplayable. Anything with higher-end graphics is a no go, you will have to drop your resolution down to 1280x720 to eek above 30-40 fps. At low settings, 1080p the best I could get from Rise of the Tomb Raider was a "steady" 20 fps. On medium settings I was getting ~30 fps on Firewatch. When it comes to gaming on the 5310 I'd say your best best is pixelated/low poly games or legacy system emulation.  PRODUCTIVITY/PERFOMANCE  Even here I wouldn't hold out high expectations for the 5310. Any sort of multi-tasking bogs down the CPU. After two weeks I've yet to have WebEx run successfully for more than two minutes before it hangs and then crashes. Web browsing is smooth, and it's only here I can appreciate the mostly unnecessary headroom offered by the 16gb of ram because I do love my multiple tabs.  Media consumption is another confusing aspect of the 5310. At times the display can be sharp and beautiful, but comparatively speaking even at max brightness it's a little dim. And the native resolution  of 2560x1600, while good for some tasks, feels awkward and an odd choice. While it's not exactly rare the 16:10 aspect ratio is a lot less common than 16:9 so expect to frequently see black bars in any gaming or media. And although I appreciate the sharpness of the QHD resolution I wonder why FusionTech chose to pair this resolution with a CPU geared towards 1080p performance?  CONCLUSION  Design wise this is a sleek little ultra-portable laptop. It's lightweight, minimalist, and a pleasure to work on with the few tasks it handles well. Unfortunately it feels like FusionTech shoved in a bunch of unnecessary, expensive, and poorly paired hardware to create this mostly unusable, mismatched Frankenstein of a machine.  And on top of all that the various FusionTech bloatware included makes this even more undesirable.

title_x: Keyboard shorts to touchpad, Earphone plug doesn't work
text_clean: Keyboard shorts to touchpad, Earphone plug doesn't work If the user interface doesn't work, all the great stuff underneath is worthless. This computer turned into an irritation. You cannot touch any part of the metal plate that holds in the keyboard and touchpad, because it shorts out to the touchpad, and then the cursor moves to someplace you didn't intend to be. If you touch a "d" key, and contact is made with the metal plate surrounding the "d" key, you end up typing in some odd place of the document. If you try to open a new browser tab using the touchpad, be careful not to touch the metal plate because you could open several other things as well. Try to double tap to highlight a word, you get a whole paragraph. This hardware problem is all over the internet. Frustrating machine. I'll get it repaired and the sell it for something that's useable.

text_clean: Killer Wifi makes this computer unusable Going on 3 years with this computer now. Plenty of power and storage. The computer does not properly sleep when the lid is closed and it will try to cook itself in a bag so be careful and always shut it down. Not a problem since boot time is very fast.  These and other model FusionTechs use a wifi card (killer) with gaming optimizations that have known issues and slow wifi speed to 2-3 Mbps when web browsing and downloading. So if you plan to use this laptop on wifi this will not be a good solution for you. If you plan to use a docking station with ethernet you should be all good.  I included a couple screen shots of speed tests from the laptop and a phone at same time to show the flaw. FusionTech is unable to help with the issue as you will see if you do a google search.

title_x: Great Laptop if the Touchpad Worked
text_clean: Great Laptop if the Touchpad Worked This review is for the FusionTech Worktop 16 7610 16 Inch Thin Laptop (Intel Core i7-11800H, 16GB DDR4 RAM, 512GB SSD, NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Graphics, Windows 10 Pro.  On paper, this was the best laptop that I could find for what I was looking for:  16" monitor, 16 GB RAM, minimum 512GB NVMe SSD, Thunderbolt, 10 key pad, and backlit keyboard.  I was strongly leaning towards Windows 10 Pro (for various reasons), which this also happened to have.  I don't do any gaming, so even the on-die GPU would have been sufficient, however I do like having the RTX 3050 if I were ever to hook up to external displays for an overall two or three display setup (including the laptop's monitor).  The 16" monitor is a big step up in screen area compared to the 15.6" monitor that I had been using for years.  I also like that this monitor has no glare and an amazing 3072 x 1920 pixel resolution.  The screen looked really good.  I was ok with the screen only getting 300 nits bright, but I wouldn't want to consider anything lower.  This laptop was surprisingly small and light, especially given the monitor size.  It was actually smaller in length, width, height, and weight than my 15.6" laptop, and that's even with this FusionTech's battery being double the capacity of my old laptop.  The physical sliding webcam shutter was a nice bonus, and I wish all laptops had a physical shutter or physical switch to shut off webcam.  The keyboard and touchpad both felt pretty good.  The keys had decent feedback (for a laptop) and the touchpad is the largest that I have seen on a laptop.  Unfortunately, the touchpad is where everything fell apart for this laptop.  The touch pad gets jittery and stops responding.  I originally purchased an open box from a local retail store that had this touchpad problem show up within just minutes of usage.  The IT center at the store also had it show up for them within a few minutes as well.  They did a full driver update, and it worked for about 20 minutes, turned off the laptop, and the next day about five minutes in and the touchpad started having the same issues again.  I then tried the brand new floor model (running Windows 11 Home), and a couple minutes of using the trackpad had the issue on that one too.  Even the manager came over and had the same issue.  He said he has seen the issue before, and he said FusionTech's answer was to replace the motherboard (including the soldered on CPU).  I found many reviews, posts, forums, even video posts, and tech channels all talking about the dreaded touchpad issue that plagues this laptop.  The tech channels showed three solutions:  change graphic hardware settings, place filler material between the battery and touchpad, or to solder on a grounding wire (voids warranty).  Ultimately, it was the touchpad issues that had me decide to return the laptop and look for something else.

During the screen replacement the tech damaged my motherboard leaving my computer down. I was not happy but accidents happen. I was with him when it happened and when he notified his company. The nightmare begins. I told the tech I was leaving over the weekend and traveling 600 miles away and needed the laptop fixed. Documents are stored on the pc and needed, while traveling my phone and tablet will not do the job. Same notice, called support. Spoke with Neil, was informed parts were in the area and I would be notified.  12-16-2021 After checking multiple times online with no ETA for parts I called again. The motherboard is back ordered. NO INFO given.  12-17-2021 41 minute call, asked for supervisor. Had to explain again my issues and why hasn't my pc been fixed. Insisted I speak with a supervisor to get the situation resolved. As most phone reps, he apologized often, was sympathetic, and could not resolve my problem as he did not have access to parts and could not authorize a replacement unit. I was promised a call back by the end of business that day, I missed the call but received the following email.  Rxxxxx,  I just left you a voicemail with this information as well but just wanted to double down on the communication, I don't have an ETA for the motherboard that we are going to replace for you yet, the matter has been escalated to my peers in charge of the shipping of this parts and units and as soon as we have an update we will communicate with you again.  Thanks.  Axxxxx Bxxxxxxxx Supervisor, Client Tech Support  12-19-2021 No change online, still no information on parts. At least FusionTech is consistent. text_clean: It overheats I cannot recommend this product, or FusionTech products in general. The G7 7790 overheats terrible out of the box. updates do not fix it. FusionTech has the CPU set to overclock by default which just causes it to shut itself off under load. I was able to make the device usable, so if people purchased it already and need some help, here are some things you can do if you are reasonably savvy. First, lower the voltage going to the CPU a bit. Undervolting and underclocking helped the heat issue. Second, reapply the thermal paste for the CPU and GPU. I disassembled it and found that they used really bad thermal paste and it wasn't applied very well. So take it apart, clean it off with some alcohol wipes, and use Arctic or Cooler Master or something better. After adjusting clocks and redoing the thermal compound, my GPU and CPU run about 70ish degrees under load. I've noticed the fans are a lot quieter and it cools down way faster after gaming.  One thing I did like about mine, is they included the ribbon cable to connect an SSD. I purchased mine with the smaller 512gb m.2, and took it apart to upgrade it, and saw that the ribbon cable was included for a second storage device which was a nice surprise.
